title: A Methodology for Selecting and Composing Runtime Architecture Patterns for Production LLM Agents
core_claim: Production LLM agent runtimes can be treated through a stochastic-deterministic boundary contract, and a small catalog of composable runtime patterns helps select architectures that improve reliability as model variance shrinks.
triage_rationale: The paper is squarely about production LLM agent architecture and offers a concrete methodology, pattern catalog, and a runnable implementation, which makes it useful for both research and episode critique. It reads as more conceptual than empirically deep, but the runtime focus and failure-mode framing are strong enough to merit review.
likely_red_flags: Single-author paper with no affiliations listed
Appears primarily conceptual; empirical validation depth is unclear from the abstract
Some terminology and framing may be author-invented rather than established
